claim: Writing a comprehensive PRD (Product Requirements Document) before building is critical to ensure the AI agent builds what you actually want
actionable: When delegating tasks to Claude Code, always write a detailed PRD first that articulates the problem, success criteria, scope, constraints, and build plan rather than jumping straight to implementation

claim: Separate context management from memory in agent systems—what the model sees vs. what survives long-term are distinct concerns
actionable: Build your agent harness so the model's current context window is managed independently from a separate memory store that persists across sessions

claim: Use a three-layer memory architecture with project hierarchy to organize agent state and outputs
actionable: Design your Claude Code workspace with three organizational layers (top-level projects, sub-projects, tasks) and implement corresponding memory files at each layer

claim: Model context window limits grow as agents accumulate conversational history, creating a scaling problem that requires strategic truncation or sub-agents
actionable: Implement memory storage outside the main context window and use sub-agents for data-intensive operations to prevent context overflow in long sessions
